summary:
Claim: Independent assortment of maternal and paternal chromosomes occurs during anaphase I of meiosis I in spermatogenesis.  
Evidence: - During anaphase I, homologous chromosomes (maternal and paternal) separate randomly into daughter cells. - This random segregation leads to genetically diverse sperm cells.  
Answer: B  

In clinical communication, complex concepts are simplified using everyday analogies (e.g., card shuffling), visual aids (diagrams, animations), or relatable examples (family traits). These tools enhance patient understanding and engagement. Integrating such explanations into prenatal counseling improves health literacy, enables informed decisions, and fosters trust by presenting genetics as accessible and personally relevant. This patient-centered approach strengthens shared decision-making and promotes lifelong wellness awareness. (498 tokens)
